rule.name: AvoidCalendarDateCreation
rule.message: Avoid creating Calendar instances; use the java.time API (LocalDateTime, ZonedDateTime) instead.
rule.description:
java.util.Calendar is a heavyweight, mutable object. Creating a Calendar instance just to obtain the current date or time is expensive and unnecessarily verbose.
Prefer the modern java.time API introduced in Java 8: - java.time.LocalDateTime.now() / ZonedDateTime.now() for date-time values - java.time.Instant.now() for a machine timestamp - java.time.LocalDate.today() for date-only values
Note: this rule requires kotlin-type-mapper pre-analysis to be available. See the pmd-kotlin documentation for setup instructions.
Kotlin equivalent of Java rule: category/java/performance.xml/AvoidCalendarDateCreation

property.name: javaEquivalentRule
property.description: Java rule this Kotlin rule is based on; update this rule when the Java rule changes.
